keyTopics: Water Analogy for Electricity — Tank (Battery), Pipe (Wire), Tap (Switch)
Resistor — Rocks in a pipe (slows current) | Hindi: Pratirodh
Potentiometer — Adjustable tap (volume control / fan regulator)
LED — Glowing fountain (electricity → light) | GLOW GLOW GLOW!
Motor — Water wheel (electricity → motion) | SPIN SPIN SPIN!
Capacitor — Bucket / storage (fills up then SPLASH!) | stores energy
Diode — One-way gate (current flows only forward)

activity.title: Breadboard LED + Resistor + Switch Circuit
steps: Connect LED — long leg to resistor
Power ON → Press switch → LED glows!
Challenge: Make LED blink using Arduino code

quiz.q: In the water analogy, what does the BATTERY represent?
options: A water tank that gives power
quiz.explanation: Battery = Water Tank! It stores and provides energy to the circuit, just like a tank provides water pressure.

quiz.q: In the water analogy, what does a WIRE represent?
quiz.explanation: Wire = Pipe! Just as pipes carry water from the tank, wires carry electrical current from battery to components.

quiz.q: What does a RESISTOR do to electric current?
options: Slows it down and limits the flow
quiz.explanation: Resistor = Rocks in a pipe! It slows down the current, protecting components from too much electricity.

options: An adjustable resistor that controls current — like a volume knob
quiz.explanation: Potentiometer = Adjustable tap! You can turn it to control how much current flows — like a fan regulator!

quiz.q: What real-life device works like a Potentiometer?
quiz.explanation: A fan speed regulator or TV volume knob is a potentiometer! Turning it adjusts current flow.

quiz.q: In the water analogy, an LED is like a:
options: Glowing fountain that lights up when water flows
quiz.explanation: LED = Glowing Fountain! When electricity flows through it, it converts energy into LIGHT. Bijli → Roshni!

quiz.q: What does a MOTOR convert electricity into?
quiz.explanation: Motor = Water Wheel! When electricity flows, it spins — converting electrical energy into movement. Bijli → Motion!

quiz.q: In the water analogy, a CAPACITOR is like a:
options: Bucket that fills up and then releases all at once
quiz.explanation: Capacitor = Bucket! It fills up with electrical charge slowly, then releases it all at once.

quiz.q: What is special about a DIODE?
options: It stores a lot of energy
It only allows current to flow in ONE direction
quiz.explanation: Diode = One-Way Gate! Electricity can pass forward ✅, but CANNOT go backward 🚫.

options: A small computer chip that controls circuits based on a program
quiz.explanation: Microcontroller = Smart Brain! A tiny computer that runs your code to control LEDs, motors, sensors.

quiz.q: What does an OPEN circuit mean?
options: The circuit loop is broken — no current flows
quiz.explanation: Open circuit = broken loop = no current flows. Like a tap that's closed — water has nowhere to go.
Resistor = Pratirodh (Hindi) / प्रतिरोध (Marathi) — means 'opposition', limiting current flow!

quiz.q: An Arduino / Raspberry Pi is an example of a:
quiz.explanation: Arduino and Raspberry Pi are microcontrollers! They're smart brains that run your programs.

quiz.q: If you put rocks in a water pipe, water flows:
quiz.explanation: Rocks in a pipe = resistor! More rocks = more resistance = slower water flow. Same with resistors!

quiz.q: What action did students do to understand SPIN SPIN SPIN?
options: Rotate their hands to act out a motor spinning
quiz.explanation: For MOTOR = Water Wheel, students rotated their hands and shouted 'SPIN SPIN SPIN!' to feel the concept!
